rationale: The citing paper uses Lim, Ahearne, and Ham (2009) repeatedly in theory framing, not just in a citation list. It cites that paper's empirical results on prize structure (multiple winners help, equal prizes can match rank-ordered prizes) and treats these findings as the benchmark for a state-of-the-art single-segment contest. The authors then build their own design around that benchmark and test a new segmentation-based contest format and mechanisms, including a no-contest control.
relation_description: The 2025 study builds on Lim et al. (2009) by taking its prize-structure conclusions as a baseline single-segment contest design and then adding segmentation as a new design element to test relative effectiveness. In this sense, it extends prior sales-contest design research from prize structure to segment-based contest architecture and mechanism testing.
